topic.what: First documented observation of spiral organisms in gastric mucosa (dogs), predating Marshall-Warren by 90 years.
topic.how: Histological examination of dog gastric tissue revealing spiral bacterial colonization.
topic.why_matters: Canonical example of paradigm-driven blindness — empirical observation suppressed by the 'sterile stomach' assumption for nearly a century.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time between initial observation and rediscovery
characteristics.vs_baseline: One of medicine's longest rediscovery gaps

novelty: First observation of gastric spiral bacteria in any species
Demonstrated stomach was not sterile — 90 years before this was accepted
open_problems: Observation was in dogs, not humans
No connection to gastritis pathology was made
